chinese_translation: 定冠词：这个/那个/这些；也可作宾语代词
example_english: long live politics, long live love
noun_pattern: before a noun or adjective + noun
usage_note: Use le before masculine singular nouns, la before feminine singular nouns, l’ before a vowel/silent h, and les before plural nouns.
memory_note: This row is mixed POS: determiner + pronoun. As a determiner, memorize it with nouns: le livre, la maison, les livres.

example_french: il ne rêve que d'argent et de plaisirs
example_english: he only dreams of money and pleasure
noun_pattern: de + noun; du/de la/de l’/des before noun phrases
usage_note: As a determiner, de appears in partitive/indefinite forms. It is also very often a preposition, so check the sentence role.
memory_note: Do not memorize de only as 'of'. In food/quantity contexts, du/de la/de l’/des often means some/any.

noun_pattern: ce + masculine noun|cet + masculine noun starting with vowel/silent h|cette + feminine noun|ces + plural noun
usage_note: used before a noun to point to this/that thing
memory_note: Determiners sit before nouns and agree with the noun: ce livre, cet homme, cette maison, ces livres.

fem_singular: ma / mon before vowel or silent h
usage_note: Possessive determiners agree with the noun owned, not with the speaker. Use mon before feminine nouns starting with a vowel/silent h.
memory_note: Think noun gender first: mon livre, ma maison, mes livres. For vowel sound: mon amie.

example_french: notre ville a le sens de la communauté
example_english: our city has a sense of community
usage_note: Use notre for singular nouns and nos for plural nouns. Same singular form for masculine and feminine nouns.
memory_note: For notre, gender does not change the singular form. Number matters: notre ville, notre livre, nos villes.

example_french: permettez-moi de vous parler dans votre langue
example_english: allow me to speak to you in your language
usage_note: Use votre for singular nouns and vos for plural nouns. Same singular form for masculine and feminine nouns.
memory_note: For votre, gender does not change the singular form. Number matters: votre langue, votre livre, vos livres.
